page: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. The instance features 96 dedicated physical cores built on the arm64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-V2 model. It operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity stands at 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The server does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top 10% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 10% for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read/write operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench image processing landing in the middle 50% tier.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates mixed capabilities. It excels in database operations, scoring in the top 10% for Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, alongside top-tier static web serving throughput. Conversely, while LLM inference text generation for medium and large models ranks in the top 25%, text generation for small models falls into the bottom 10%. This bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and cost efficiency for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or local storage-heavy workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong software compilation and static web serving capabilities. However, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation represent notable performance tradeoffs, scoring in the average and bottom tiers respectively. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 arm64 Graviton cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A bare-metal arm64 server featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and DDR5 memory for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads and database operations.

384.0 GB of high-bandwidth DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core
